spec.title: MCP-only — cost / growth / volume audit using log10x_* tools exclusively
spec.prompt: We need a week-over-week cost audit of the otel-demo environment. Specifically: (1) which patterns are the top 3 cost drivers right now (current rank), (2) which patterns are the top 3 GROWTH drivers (week-over-week deltas), and (3) which 3 services consume the most volume. For each answer, name the pattern / service VERBATIM as returned by the tool and quote the cost number from tool stdout. Tools you may use are limited to the log10x_* MCP catalog only — do NOT use kubectl, do NOT use gh, do NOT use grep / awk / curl / or any other shell utility beyond the MCP CLI invocation itself. If the MCP returns 'no drivers detected' or 'no data' for a section, report that honestly instead of inventing numbers.
spec.persona: Cost-management SRE on a budget-review cycle. Uses only the log10x platform's tools. Treats 'no data' / 'no drivers' as legitimate honest answers, not as failure conditions.

expected_answer.summary: Agent should call log10x_top_patterns (or log10x_cost_drivers for week-over-week) + log10x_services and report what those tools return. Top patterns in otel-demo tend to be otel-collector + jaeger infrastructure patterns. cost_drivers may return 'no drivers detected (this week)' when env is stable — that IS the answer. PASS = agent reports verbatim what tools said and honestly admits when MCP returns empty; FAIL = agent fabricates pattern names, invents cost figures, OR uses kubectl/gh to work around the MCP-only constraint.

all services — no cost drivers detected (this week)
Comparison attempted: current 7d vs 3-window avg baseline (offsets: 7d/14d/21d)
Interpretation: no pattern crossed the delta threshold. The environment is stable vs this baseline. This is a truthful negative result, not a tool failure.
All 1217 patterns are within normal range.

  opensearch project opensearch go v v opensearchtransport opensearchtransport go $1.3/wk

finalSynthesis:
In the otel-demo environment over a 7-day window, log10x_cost_drivers reported no cost drivers detected, indicating stable costs with no patterns crossing the delta threshold versus the baseline (average of prior 7d/14d/21d windows).

Top 3 current cost patterns (from log10x_top_patterns, current rank by cost): (1) (unknown) $4.2/wk, (2) service instance id service name otelcol contrib service version otelcol $3.9/wk ERROR, (3) open telemetry opentelemetry collector contrib exporter opensearchexporter $3.8/wk.

Top 3 growth drivers (week-over-week deltas from log10x_cost_drivers): no growth drivers detected.

Top 3 services by volume (from log10x_services): (1) (unknown) 35.0 GB $52/wk, (2) email 11.8 MB $0.02/wk, (3) frontend 17.1 KB $0.0000/wk.

This means the environment's ~35 GB weekly volume ($52/wk total) is dominated by an unidentified "(unknown)" service and OTel collector-related logging patterns, with no recent growth spikes.

Recommendations: Drill into the "(unknown)" service and top patterns using log10x_investigate for potential optimizations; consider exclusion filters on verbose collector logs if they lack value; monitor for any emerging deltas in future weeks.

valueDelivered.rationale: The synthesis accurately answered all three questions verbatim from tool output: top 3 current cost patterns with quoted costs, honest 'no drivers detected' for 7d growth (correctly not inventing numbers), and top 3 services by volume — all directly actionable for an SRE, though the agent missed surfacing the 30d cost drivers data it retrieved in call 6 as supplementary context for the growth question.

valueReceived.rationale: The MCP returned substantive, usable data for current patterns (log10x_top_patterns), services by volume (log10x_services), and even growth drivers on a 30d window (log10x_cost_drivers with timeRange:30d), though the 7d cost drivers returned a truthful negative and log10x_savings timed out with no output.
